name: TabbyAPI
pricing_notes: Open source (Apache 2.0); self-hosted on NVIDIA GPU

performance.notes: High-speed inference via ExLlamaV2 backend; among fastest local serving for GPTQ/EXL2 models

agent_readiness.notes: OpenAI-compatible local LLM server powered by ExLlamaV2. Self-hosted on NVIDIA GPU. Supports GPTQ, EXL2, and safetensors models. OpenAI API compatibility means any OpenAI SDK works. Fast streaming. Admin API for model loading/unloading. Used by power users running multiple quantized models on consumer hardware.

gotchas: Smaller community than Ollama or LM Studio
No model download management — must manually provide models
Documentation less polished than commercial alternatives
best_when: Power users needing maximum performance for EXL2/GPTQ models
OpenAI-compatible local API for coding assistants
Consumer GPU setups (RTX 3090/4090) with quantized models
When ExLlamaV2 performance is specifically needed
avoid_when: Multi-GPU or cloud deployments (vLLM or TGI better)
summary: TabbyAPI is an OpenAI-compatible local LLM server powered by ExLlamaV2, offering maximum inference speed for GPTQ and EXL2 quantized models on consumer NVIDIA GPUs. Any OpenAI SDK works with it. Supports fast streaming and admin API for model management. Best for power users with 24GB+ VRAM cards who need top performance for quantized models.
